one_time_description: Large capital project deferred revenues received: ISC Vogel Padakus Creek ($5,342,304) and ISC Hydro Roads upgrade ($701,328)
economic_notes: First Nation participates in Musgamagw Dzawada'enuxw Fisheries Group Limited Partnership (33.33% share) focused on marine activities and economic development in Musgamagw Territory. Also has Dzawada'enuxw Development Corporation. Defined contribution pension plan in place with 5.5% employer match.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED: auditors could not obtain sufficient evidence to support deferral of $14,477,633 in deferred revenue; the entire deferred revenue balance may be overstated and revenue/surplus may be understated. Deferred revenue balance jumped from $7,028,916 to $14,592,757 largely due to new capital project funding (ISC Vogel Padakus Creek $5,342,304 and ISC Hydro Roads $701,328). Two entities accounted for by modified equity method (Musgamagw Dzawada'enuxw Fisheries Group LP and Dzawada'enuxw Development Corporation) are excluded from consolidation. CMHC Social Housing Program is consolidated. Long-term debt fully repaid during the year. Province classified as BC based on auditor location and nation description. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,785,113 + FNHA $1,180,358 + Province of BC $263,898 + CMHC $110,038 + FNESC $80,987 + NVIAATS $20,957 + Ottawa Trust $2,174 = $4,443,525; OSR = Other revenue $997,919 + Rental $48,310 + Interest $20,545 + Partnership income $61,042 = $1,127,816. Revenue lines sum to $5,571,341.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
